page: Vultr vbm-256c-2048gb-8-mi300x-gpu is a bare metal server configured for high-density compute and accelerator-heavy workloads. It features 256 dedicated AMD EPYC 9534 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.45 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core to support large-scale parallel processing tasks.

The primary accelerator hardware consists of eight AMD MI300X GPUs built on the CDNA3 architecture, delivering a combined total of 1536 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. For local data storage, the server includes 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, facilitating rapid read and write operations directly on the bare metal hardware. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is included with the deployment.

This hardware profile is engineered for workloads requiring massive parallel computation and large memory footprints, such as large language model training, high-performance computing simulations, and complex data science pipelines. The bare metal deployment model eliminates virtualization overhead, ensuring dedicated access to all CPU, memory, and GPU resources.
description: Vultr vbm-256c-2048gb-8-mi300x-gpu is a bare metal server designed for intensive computational workloads. It is equipped with 256 dedicated AMD EPYC 9534 cores running at 2.45 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 2048.0 GB of system memory. The server features eight AMD MI300X GPUs from the CDNA3 family, providing a total of 1536 GB of VRAM to accelerate parallel processing tasks. Local storage is provided by 28608 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, ensuring high-speed data access without virtualization overhead. This configuration is optimized for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, and high-performance computing applications that demand substantial memory and GPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vbm-256c-2048gb-8-mi300x-gpu is a bare metal server featuring 256 AMD EPYC cores, 2048 GB RAM, 28608 GB NVMe SSD storage, and eight AMD MI300X GPUs with 1536 GB total VRAM for intensive AI and HPC workloads.

tagline: A bare metal accelerator platform featuring eight AMD MI300X GPUs, two hundred fifty-six dedicated EPYC cores, and massive NVMe storage.
